metrics.narrative: Amresh Munshi named directional negotiation parameters on the 19 Aug 2025 call (about 10.5 months ago) — a $25k-$50k autonomous-sourcing threshold, Net 45/60 payment-term targets for new vs. existing vendors, and a 3-5% cap on annual renewal increases — but no dollar-quantified ROI or savings model has ever been built to justify the $185,000 investment. No update since.

champion.narrative: Amresh Munshi was an engaged, technically curious champion on the 19 Aug 2025 call, personally probing integration mechanics and committing to coordinate a broader September 2025 session with senior stakeholders — but that follow-up never happened, and he has been unresponsive since his last confirmed email reply on 26 Feb 2026 (about 4.5 months ago), despite rep re-engagement attempts on 28 Apr, 13 May, 1 Jun, 22 Jun, and 29 Jun 2026. No relationship exists with Purav Desai, Chris Soriano, Alycia Stafford, or Justin Robinson beyond their listing as contact roles.

competition.narrative: GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are named as the active bake-off (Competitors__c), consistent with Amresh Munshi's 19 Aug 2025 statement that Cornerstone was 'exploring all the providers.' Oracle iProcurement is confirmed as an entrenched, non-displaceable incumbent (overlay target only), and Coupa was rejected by Cornerstone leadership in 2021 and is not part of this evaluation. No competitive intelligence has surfaced since the planned Dec 2025/Jan 2026 down-select window passed.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and well-articulated. Amresh Munshi described unmanaged tail spend under the $25k-$50k threshold handled entirely outside any system ('we do everything outside the system, but via email'), a real security incident where a ~$10,000 tool was purchased without a required TSR review, and frustration that Oracle iProcurement 'is behind in enhancing' AI capability at 'significantly high cost.' He also called Cornerstone's 2021 decision to move from NetSuite/Coupa to the relevant stakeholder 'a mistake.'

sources: No evidence found across Next Step, Next Step History, or Tasks
paper_process.narrative: No SOW, MSA, or Order Form discussion has occurred at any point; the deal has never progressed to vendor selection or contracting.

economic_buyer.narrative: Amresh Munshi stated on 19 Aug 2025 that his Head of Global Procurement would need to 'give his blessing and take it forward' before any deal proceeds, but that person has never joined a call, been named, or been contacted directly in the roughly 10.5 months since. No budget-holder conversation has started.

decision_process.narrative: Amresh Munshi laid out a full process on 19 Aug 2025: vendor exploration through September 2025, down-select, a POC by December 2025/January 2026, contracting after the POC, and a go-live target around May 2026 (aggressive) or within a year (safer). That plan appears to have collapsed — the stage was demoted from Shortlisted back to the relevant stakeholder on 14 Apr 2026, indicating the process reset rather than advanced.

decision_criteria.narrative: Concrete criteria surfaced on the 19 Aug 2025 call: an overlay solution that does not replace Oracle iProcurement, configurable autonomous-sourcing dollar thresholds, negotiation-tone configurability, SSO via Okta/SAML, and diverse-supplier (ESG) network integration. No formal BRD or RFP has confirmed or refreshed this list since.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Formal Evaluation deal that reached genuine technical depth in a single rich discovery/demo call on 19 Aug 2025 (about 10.5 months ago) but has been effectively dark on the buyer side since a 26 Feb 2026 email reply (about 4.5 months ago). Single-threaded through a now-unresponsive mid-level champion, no economic buyer engaged, no quantified business case, and no confirmed standing in the four-way competitive bake-off.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through a now-unresponsive mid-level champion with no economic buyer, no budget conversation, and a $185,000 ask with no quantified ROI or savings case behind it.

win_position.text: Champion gone quiet — Amresh Munshi's last reply was an email on 26 Feb 2026, and five rep outreach attempts since (28 Apr, 13 May, 1 Jun, 22 Jun, 29 Jun 2026) have gone unanswered, per Next_Step_History__c.
No economic buyer engaged — the Head of Global Procurement was named by the champion on 19 Aug 2025 as needing to give final approval, but has never joined a call or been contacted directly.

Genuine early depth exists — the 19 Aug 2025 call ran nearly an hour with the champion probing autonomous-sourcing thresholds, negotiation configurability, and Oracle/ServiceNow integration in real detail, evidencing authentic technical fit exploration.

Competitive standing unconfirmed — GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba were named as the shortlist with a planned Dec 2025/Jan 2026 POC down-select that has passed with zero visibility into where Zycus stands.

deal_momentum.text: Rep outreach into silence — Next_Step__c logged 'no material update' on 1 Jun and 29 Jun 2026, and a follow-up to the champion on 22 Jun 2026 drew no response.
Stage regressed — the opportunity was demoted from Shortlisted back to Formal Evaluation on 14 Apr 2026, reversing the only forward stage movement this deal had made.
Close date repeatedly slipping — the close date has moved at least nine times over roughly the past eleven months (from 27 Oct 2025 to the current 27 Aug 2026), per field history on record.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer-committed September 2025 follow-up call with senior stakeholders, promised by the champion on 19 Aug 2025, was never scheduled.

No stakeholder beyond the champion has ever engaged directly with Zycus — Purav Desai, Chris Soriano, Alycia Stafford, and Justin Robinson remain contact-role names only, with no calls or replies on record.
